coreProductOrService: After over 11 years as Chief Risk Officer (CRO), I am excited to share that I have made the decision to retire from FIS . I’d like to thank my fellow FIS teammates, our clients, regulators and Board members, present and past, for what has been a fantastic career experience. I will miss the incredibly talented people from all over the globe and all that we have accomplished together for our clients and communities. I leave with great memories working with fellow industry leaders at the Financial Services - Information Sharing and Analysis Center ( FS-ISAC ) and its Sheltered Harbor initiative, the Internet Security Alliance (ISA) and the American Transaction Processors Coalition (ATPC), as well as community leaders from Catholic Charities Jacksonville (CCBJAX), United Way of Northeast Florida (UWNEFL), Metrolina Association for the Blind (MAB), the Cathedral Arts Project (CAP) and the American Heart Association (AHA). I am also proud to have founded the FIS Disability Inclusion Network, which will continue with the support of wonderful leaders across the company. Finally, I’d like to thank my wife, Karen, and our three children for their support and love over the last 25 years. I am truly blessed and looking forward to this next exciting chapter of life.
